item.title: DOJ Signals Antitrust Shift on Media Deals as AI Alters Industry
item.description: Rapid changes in media fueled by increased use of artificial intelligence and streaming services require a “cautious humility” from antitrust enforcers as they assess whether mergers in the industry are threatening competition and consumers, a senior US Justice Department official said.

item.title: Oil Slips as Iran Set to Attend Negotiations With US in Pakistan
item.description: Oil slipped on signs that Iran will attend negotiations with the US in Islamabad before a ceasefire between the sides ends.

item.title: Iran Tensions Flare Again: What’s Next for Negotiations?
item.description: The US and Iran are still at odds over the future of Tehran’s nuclear program and control of the Strait of Hormuz. What that friction could mean for peace talks and a looming ceasefire deadline.

item.title: Read Memos From Tim Cook and John Ternus on Apple CEO Transition
item.description: After tapping hardware chief John Ternus to succeed Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook as the iPhone maker’s leader, Apple Inc. sent memos to staff from both executives explaining the move.

item.title: Amazon to Invest an Additional $5 Billion in Anthropic
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. is investing an additional $5 billion in Anthropic PBC, and may inject $20 billion more over time, a deal that deepens the companies’ ties in an increasingly competitive artificial intelligence industry.

item.title: Apple Names Ternus as Next CEO; Tim Cook to Become Chairman
item.description: Apple Inc. named hardware chief John Ternus as the iPhone maker’s next leader, with Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook shifting to the role of executive chairman.

item.title: South America Could Unlock 2.1 Million Barrels a Day on $100 Oil
item.description: South America could deliver an additional 2.1 million barrels of daily oil supplies by 2035 with crude prices around $100, Rystad Energy wrote in note on Monday.

item.title: Inside Alex Cooper’s Unwell: Tears, Screaming and Employees Looking for the Exit
item.description: The Unwell Network’s head of brand marketing, head of the network and chief growth officer have departed in the past year amid staff turnover and a sputtering slate of shows

item.title: Diplo, Twenty One Pilots Royalties to Back Bonds Sold by Chord
item.description: Chord Music Partners, a firm which invests in music rights, is selling $500 million of bonds backed by royalties from artists including Twenty One Pilots, Diplo and Morgan Wallen.

item.title: Stock Market’s Rally to Record High Masks Weak Building Blocks
item.description: The record-breaking advance in US stocks has many of the hallmarks of an unloved rally, including narrow leadership, low volumes and muted investor sentiment.

item.title: How Trump’s Fed Drama Risks Backfiring — and Keeping Powell in Power
item.description: President Donald Trump has nominated Kevin Warsh, a former Federal Reserve governor, to succeed Jerome Powell when his term as chair of the Fed’s Board of Governors expires on May 15. Typically, new chairs are subjected to plenty of scrutiny, but the transition of power is almost always smooth and uncontroversial. Not so this time.

item.title: Your Questions About Anthropic’s Mythos AI Model, Answered
item.description: Bloomberg reporters answered dozens of questions from readers about the new model in a Live Q&A conversation

item.title: WhatsApp Joins Instagram in Selling New Premium Subscriptions
item.description: WhatsApp is selling a new premium subscription that includes more custom features, part of a broader effort by parent company Meta Platforms Inc. to build a revenue stream that can complement its core advertising business.

item.title: Romania Coalition Nears Collapse as Top Party Spurns Premier
item.description: Romania’s government has been thrown into turmoil after the top ruling party withdrew its support for Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan, whose own bloc is now rallying to salvage his control of the four-way coalition.

item.title: Sunshine Silver Weighs $400 Million IPO to Fund Reopening of Idaho Mine
item.description: Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining Corp. is pursuing an initial public offering to help reopen an Idaho mine, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a pipeline of mining listings as elevated metals prices draw investor interest.

item.title: Bank of Canada Adds Gosselin, Vincent to Full-Time Rate-Setting Council
item.description: The Bank of Canada has selected two new deputies for full-time positions on its rate-setting governing council.

item.title: LA Children’s Hospital Taps Muni Market as It Contends With Medicaid Cuts
item.description: The Children’s Hospital Los Angeles is planning to sell $187.5 million of federally taxable municipal revenue bonds as it contends with financial pressure tied to federal and state cuts to California’s Medicaid program.

item.title: Drone Maker Aevex Shares Double in Just Two Days After Debut
item.description: Military drone manufacturer Aevex Corp.’s shares have doubled in two trading sessions, the latest among recent defense technology firms making a big splash upon market entry.

item.title: Bad Bunny evitó pérdidas en conciertos de Medellín con seguro personalizado contra el mal clima
item.description: Bad Bunny tenía un problema. Días antes de que la megaestrella puertorriqueña se presentara en Colombia anteriormente este año, la posibilidad de fuertes lluvias amenazaba con que tres conciertos agotados en Medellín se convirtieran en pérdidas multimillonarias.

item.title: Spirit Floats US Government Stake to Avoid Potential Liquidation
item.description: Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc. has floated offering the US government an equity stake in the discount carrier to help stave off its potential liquidation, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Citi Sees Oil at $110 If Hormuz Disruption Lasts Another Month
item.description: Oil prices could rise to $110 per barrel if traffic in the Strait of Hormuz remains disrupted for another month, according to Citigroup.

item.title: We’ve Seen Some Truly Historic Moves in Markets
item.description: Investors have rarely dealt with this before.

item.title: Even Big Profits Yield Small Stock Pops in Market Fixated on War
item.description: One week into the latest quarterly earnings season, even companies that are clearing Wall Street’s already high bar are seeing little in the way of stock-price jumps — another testament to how much the market is hanging on the US-Iran war.

item.title: BofA Says Buy US Treasuries That Have Lagged Broader Iran Rally
item.description: US Treasuries are still pricing in a war in the Middle East that other asset classes have shrugged off, setting up a drop in yields as they catch up, according to Bank of America.

item.title: Direct Lending Fundraising Hits Three-Year Low in First Quarter
item.description: Direct-lending strategy funds raised a total of about $10.7 billion in funds completed during the first quarter, the lowest quarterly amount recorded in the past three years, according to data compiled by With Intelligence, an alternative investment research provider owned by S&P Global.

item.title: Polymarket in Talks for New Investment at $15 Billion Valuation
item.description: Polymarket is seeking an additional $400 million in funding, according to people familiar with the negotiations, after securing $600 million at a $15 billion valuation last month.

item.title: Fertitta Extends Talks for $18 Billion Takeover of Caesars
item.description: Caesars Entertainment Inc. has extended the period of exclusive talks about an $18 billion takeover by Tilman Fertitta as new details about the deal emerge.

item.title: NASA’s New Space Suits May Not Be Ready by 2028 Moon Landing
item.description: NASA may not have new lunar spacesuits ready by the time the agency hopes to land humans back on the moon in 2028, according to a new US government audit.

item.title: Musk Snubs French in Case Over Grok Sexual Images, AFP Says
item.description: Elon Musk snubbed a summons by French prosecutors investigating how AI chatbot Grok was allowed to spew out sexually explicit deepfakes and Holocaust-denying content, according to Agence France-Presse.

item.title: Amazon’s Alleged Price-Fixing Targets Walmart, Home Depot, Chewy
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. coerced Levi Strauss & Co. and other prominent brands into an alleged price-fixing scheme that influenced costs for a wide variety of goods on retail websites operated by Walmart Inc., Target Corp., Home Depot Inc., Chewy Inc. and others, according to court documents unsealed Monday.
